## [12:09 - 15:09]

So take that action. And finally, the third shift to really embracing the entrepreneur mindset is locus of control. You might want to write that down, locus of control. And that is the degree in which people believe that they have control over the outcome of their lives, not outside forces. And there's two ways to break this down. Number one, people with an internal locus of control, they believe I have control over my life. I am responsible for the outcomes of my life. I control my destiny. But the second way of having this control is people with an external locus of control. And what they believe is they believe others control their life and the outcomes. They believe they're a victim to their circumstances. And they are at the mercy of fate or luck and don't have the power to change what happens to them. And here's what I know. You cannot be a successful entrepreneur with an external locus of control. As an entrepreneur, it's one person who personally takes on the risk and reward of business ownership. Not just as an entrepreneur. To be fulfilled as a person, you need to have an internal locus of control. And I get it, right? It might feel scary to first take complete responsibility for your life.
